title: Where did Rei get her last name
display_title: Where did Rei get her last name
wikitext:
[]: Where did Rei get her last name?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Dec 08, 2009 1:55 pm Topic. I know the obvious answer was to fit in with the battleship naming theme, but was there an in-story reason she has the name Ayanami?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 08, 2009 2:43 pm Nope.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Dec 08, 2009 3:48 pm For a slightly more in depth explanation, there is more information on Rei's name on this page . Rei's second name appears to be part of the maritime/ship motif used for many names in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Dec 08, 2009 4:31 pm Well, Rei's cover story is that she's "the child of an acquaintance of [Gendo's]", so he probably picked the name from a dead friend of his who wouldn't be able to object.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Dec 08, 2009 9:55 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: For a slightly more in depth explanation, there is more information on Rei's name on this page .

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed Dec 09, 2009 12:53 am AuraTwilight wrote: Well, Rei's cover story is that she's "the child of an acquaintance of [Gendo's]", so he probably picked the name from a dead friend of his who wouldn't be able to object.
